Title: Structure-based engineering of substrate specificity for pinoresinol-lariciresinol reductases

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLR/PrR family; NADPH-dependent reductase; conserved GXXGXXG NADPH-binding motif
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: entry point; midstream sequential reduction steps
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces pinoresinol to lariciresinol and subsequently reduces lariciresinol to secoisolariciresinol using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product; also converts target product further downstream depending on substrate specificity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lariciresinol is a pharmaceutically valuable lignan and major bioactive component associated with antibacterial, antiviral, and immune-regulatory effects; lignans also have anticancer, antioxidant, antiviral, and immunosuppressive activities in humans
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: RNA isolated from leaves for cloning; prior work cited it as a key enzyme in lariciresinol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7/IPTG-inducible pET-duet-1 in E. coli for heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystal structures in apo/substrate/product-bound states; recombinant enzyme assays; site-directed mutagenesis; kinetic analysis; LC-MS metabolite profiling; E. coli bioconversion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IiPLR1 functions as a homodimeric NADPH-dependent reductase that converts pinoresinol to lariciresinol and then lariciresinol to secoisolariciresinol. 2. Structural analysis identified residues around the substrate pocket, especially Val46 and Ser98, as determinants of substrate specificity. 3. Structure-guided mutagenesis altered substrate preference; IiPLR1_V46A enhanced the first reduction while largely eliminating the second, increasing lariciresinol accumulation in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type IiPLR1 efficiently produces lariciresinol and secoisolariciresinol; mutant V46A promotes lariciresinol accumulation and reduces secoisolariciresinol formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step / pivotal enzyme at the entry point for multiple lignan subclasses
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with NADPH at 30°C; heterologous production in E. coli with IPTG induction and eugenol-fed precursor module
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Homodimer partner protomer; in bioconversion module coupled with Prx02 and PsVAO in pinoresinol-producing E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent metabolic engineering target to overproduce lariciresinol; structure-guided editing of IiPLR1, especially V46A, can reduce undesired conversion to secoisolariciresinol and improve microbial fermentation or gene-editing strategies in Isatis indigotica
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants that improved lariciresinol selectivity in vitro did not always increase lariciresinol in vivo, likely due to metabolic network complexity, feedback, and NADPH supply constraints
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic pocket is formed by elements from both monomers; β4 loop and residue 98 govern substrate specificity; structural deposits include PDB 7CS2-7CS8

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLR/PrR family; NADPH-dependent reductase; conserved GXXGXXG NADPH-binding motif
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 8-8′ lignan entry step with restricted second reduction
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: entry/midstream reductive step with preference for first reaction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Primarily reduces pinoresinol to lariciresinol and shows weak activity converting lariciresinol to secoisolariciresinol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms lariciresinol; weakly forms downstream secoisolariciresinol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lignans have multiple human health-associated bioactivities; lariciresinol is pharmaceutically valuable
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: RNA isolated from leaves for cloning
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7/IPTG-inducible pET-duet-1 in E. coli for heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystal structures; recombinant enzyme assays; site-directed mutagenesis; LC-MS product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPrR1 is a PrR with strong preference for pinoresinol and only weak activity toward lariciresinol. 2. Structural comparison with IiPLR1 implicated Leu46 and Asn98/β4 loop conformation in restricting lariciresinol utilization. 3. Mutations L46A/L46V and N98A/N98S increased ability to produce secoisolariciresinol, demonstrating switchable substrate specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of pinoresinol to lariciresinol but poorly supports conversion of lariciresinol to secoisolariciresinol; engineered mutants partially relieve this restriction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of branch entry/product outcome, but not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful template for engineering PLR/PrR specificity by modifying pocket residues and β4 loop dynamics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineering may alter product spectrum and downstream lignan balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: β4 loop is well defined in substrate/product-bound structures and more restrictive than in IiPLR1; structural deposits include PDB 7CS9-7CSF

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLR/PrR family; NADPH-dependent reductase; conserved GXXGXXG NADPH-binding motif
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 8-8′ lignan entry step with blocked second reduction
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: entry/midstream reductive step specialized for first reaction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces pinoresinol to lariciresinol but wild type lacks detectable activity toward lariciresinol to form secoisolariciresinol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms lariciresinol; does not normally convert lariciresinol further
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lignans are bioactive plant metabolites with pharmaceutical relevance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: RNA isolated from leaves for cloning
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7/IPTG-inducible pET-duet-1 in E. coli for heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystal structures; recombinant enzyme assays; site-directed mutagenesis; LC-MS product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPrR2 is a strict pinoresinol reductase with no detectable activity toward lariciresinol in wild type. 2. Structural comparison indicated that the β4 loop residue Asn98 and pocket geometry restrict the second reduction step. 3. Mutation N98S conferred lariciresinol-utilizing activity and enabled low but detectable secoisolariciresinol production, proving that substrate specificity is switchable.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild type only supports pinoresinol-to-lariciresinol conversion; N98S mutant gains capacity to convert lariciresinol to secoisolariciresinol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of whether the second lignan reduction occurs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong model for engineering gain-of-function specificity switches in reductases; informs design of selective lignan biosynthetic modules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutational gain of second-step activity was modest; altered pocket geometry may also affect first-step efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtPrR2 dimer orientation further condenses the substrate pocket; structural deposits include PDB 7CSG-7CSH
